headline: Trump's ballroom obsession worries Republicans
description: The costly and monumental real estate project has repeatedly been mentioned in presidential speeches, highlighting the billionaire's disconnect from public concerns. Only 28% of citizens deemed the construction necessary.

Inside a Texas migrant detention center: 'They treat us like animals'
description: At the border between the US and Mexico, federal immigration authorities have opened a massive detention site with 5,000 beds on the military grounds of Fort Bliss. A former detainee recounts the hell she endured behind those walls.

headline: Fire broke out after drone strike near UAE's Barakah nuclear plant
description: The bombing did not result in any injuries or cause a rise in radiation levels, according to the Abu Dhabi local government's media office.

headline: Elon Musk criticizes Christopher Nolan for casting Lupita Nyong'o as Helen of Troy
description: The billionaire accused the filmmaker of 'defiling Homer' by choosing a Black actress, claiming Nolan only did so to comply with diversity requirements and win an Oscar. The controversy echoes the Trump administration's opposition to affirmative action policies.

headline: Alya Aglan, historian: 'De Gaulle is difficult to interpret; he is himself an actor'
description: More than half a century after his death, General de Gaulle continues to inspire filmmakers, cartoonists and comic book authors. In an interview with Le Monde, historian Alya Aglan unpacks the driving forces behind these diverse portrayals.

description: In Corsica, chess players began learning the game since they were children, with the island counting 16 times more registered players per capita than the rest of France.

headline: A conversation with Peter Jackson, the genius tinkerer turned blockbuster master
description: The director of 'The Lord of the Rings,' who was honored with an honorary Palme d'Or, reflects on his beginnings as a self-taught filmmaker in New Zealand.

headline: After the 2025 disaster, Macron hopes to make his last year as president count
description: Emmanuel Macron, who will leave office on May 13, 2027, seeks to be remembered as a president who stayed active until the end. On the world stage, he recently regained some of the credit he lost to domestic challenges.
